sections.paragraph: Βαβυλών, ῶνος, ἡ [Heb.; the Assyrian form Babili for the capital of Babylonia was popularly interpreted as ‘Gate of the Gods’] Babylon the imperial city Mt 1:11f, 17; Ac 7:43. As symbol of oppressive religious, economic, and political power Rv 14:8; 18:10, 21; in 1 Pt 5:13 probably a code word for Rome.
